original_final_question: Across years 2015-16 through 2018-19, which Texas counties are consistently in the top five for total lunch serves in the Seamless Summer Option program? Among those counties, identify the two with the largest percentage decreases in day care home meal reimbursements from 2018-19 to 2020-21. For those two counties, compute the average child center enrollment across 2018-19 and 2019-20 and report the smaller average enrollment value. Return the number only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
reasoning_chain_text: 1. Identify the Texas counties in the top five for SSO lunch serves in program year 2015-2016.
2. Identify the Texas counties in the top five for SSO lunch serves in program year 2016-2017.
3. Identify the Texas counties in the top five for SSO lunch serves in program year 2017-2018.
4. Identify the Texas counties in the top five for SSO lunch serves in program year 2018-2019 and keep the counties that appear in all four yearly lists.
5. For the counties remaining after step 4, get their 2018-2019 day care home reimbursement totals.
6. For those same counties, get their 2020-2021 day care home reimbursement totals and determine which two have the largest percentage decreases across steps 5 and 6.
7. For the two counties identified in step 6, get their 2018-2019 child center enrollment totals.
8. For those same counties, get their 2019-2020 child center enrollment totals and return the smaller average enrollment across steps 7 and 8.
original_reasoning_chain: Node 1 (2015-2016): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Harris, Hidalgo, Dallas, Cameron, El Paso}
Node 2 (2016-2017): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 3 (2017-2018): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Cameron, Bexar}
Node 4 (2018-2019): top five LunchTotal counties -> {Hidalgo, Harris, Dallas, Bexar, Cameron}
